fields_json: {"title":"Duty drawback is leaving money on the table","slug":"duty-drawback-money-on-the-table","excerpt":"Why most importers who qualify for drawback never claim it, and how to start.","body":"If you import goods and later export them, you may be owed a refund of up to 99 percent of the duty you paid. Most companies never file because the matching work looks daunting. It is not as bad as it looks, and the refunds add up.","author":"North Star Creative","publishDate":"2026-01-23"}

{"title":"Reading your HTS code without losing your mind","slug":"reading-your-hts-code-without-losing-your-mind","excerpt":"A beginner's walk through the structure of a Harmonized Tariff Schedule number.","body":"The HTS number is not random. Once you understand chapters, headings, and subheadings, you can sanity-check the code on your own entries. We break down a real ten-digit number piece by piece.","author":"North Star Creative","publishDate":"2026-03-11"}

{"title":"What the 2026 Section 301 review means for importers","slug":"what-section-301-review-means-for-importers","excerpt":"A plain look at the latest Section 301 tariff review and what importers should check before their next shipment.","body":"The latest Section 301 review puts several product lines back in play. If you import from China, this is the time to confirm your HTS codes and check whether an exclusion applies to you. We walk through the practical steps, no panic required.","author":"North Star Creative","publishDate":"2026-02-16"}

{"title":"USMCA three years in, what changed for Ohio importers","slug":"usmca-three-years-in-ohio-importers","excerpt":"How the USMCA rules of origin have played out for Ohio manufacturers and parts importers.","body":"USMCA replaced NAFTA, but the rules of origin are stricter and the paperwork is real. We look at how Ohio auto-parts and ag importers have adjusted and where companies still get tripped up on regional value content.","author":"North Star Creative","publishDate":"2026-01-14"}

{"title":"How to prepare for a CBP focused assessment","slug":"how-to-prepare-for-a-cbp-focused-assessment","excerpt":"A short guide to getting your import program ready before CBP comes calling.","body":"A focused assessment is CBP's deep look at your import program. The companies that come through it cleanly are the ones that kept good records and tested their own classifications first. Here is what to have ready.","author":"North Star Creative","publishDate":"2026-02-27"}

{"title":"Five classification mistakes that cost importers money","slug":"five-classification-mistakes-that-cost-money","excerpt":"The HTS errors we see most often and what they cost when CBP catches them.","body":"Most duty overpayments and penalties trace back to a handful of classification mistakes: using a supplier's code, ignoring the chapter notes, or never revisiting a code that was set years ago. Here is how each one happens and how to avoid it.","author":"North Star Creative","publishDate":"2026-03-08"}

{"question":"Where is Kim Trade Services located?","answer":"Our office is in the downtown Columbus business district. We serve importers across Ohio and work with clients nationwide by phone and email.","category":"Working with us","displayOrder":9}

{"question":"How long does it take to clear a shipment?","answer":"Most properly documented entries clear the same day or the next business day. Holds happen when paperwork is incomplete or another government agency is involved.","category":"Filings","displayOrder":6}

{"question":"Can I get a refund of duties I already paid?","answer":"Often yes. If you export or destroy imported goods, duty drawback can recover up to 99 percent of what you paid. We review your records and tell you whether a claim is worth filing.","category":"Tariffs and duties","displayOrder":4}

{"question":"Do you work with small importers or only large ones?","answer":"Both. We work with first-time importers and with companies running steady container volume. The monthly retainer is built for importers who want trade support without hiring staff.","category":"Working with us","displayOrder":8}

{"question":"Do my products qualify for USMCA duty-free treatment?","answer":"It depends on where the materials come from and how much value is added in North America. We run the rule-of-origin tests and document the result before you claim any preference.","category":"Trade agreements","displayOrder":5}

{"question":"Do I need a licensed customs broker to import?","answer":"You can file your own entries, but most importers use a broker to avoid costly mistakes. Kim Trade Services holds US Customs Broker License #28471 and files on your behalf.","category":"Getting started","displayOrder":1}

{"question":"What records do I have to keep as an importer?","answer":"CBP requires you to keep entry records for five years from the date of entry. Our recordkeeping checklist walks through exactly what to retain.","category":"Compliance","displayOrder":7}

{"question":"What does it cost to work with you?","answer":"Most services are flat project fees, listed on each service page. Ongoing support is a monthly retainer starting at $1,500. We give you a quote before any work begins.","category":"Working with us","displayOrder":10}

{"question":"What is an ISF and when is it due?","answer":"The Importer Security Filing, or 10 plus 2, must reach CBP at least 24 hours before your cargo is loaded onto the vessel overseas. A late or missing ISF carries a $5,000 penalty.","category":"Filings","displayOrder":3}

{"question":"How is duty calculated on my imports?","answer":"Duty is based on the HTS classification of your goods, their customs value, and their country of origin. Get any of the three wrong and you either overpay or expose yourself to a penalty.","category":"Tariffs and duties","displayOrder":2}

{"title":"Trade Compliance Audit","slug":"trade-compliance-audit","summary":"We review your import program the way CBP would, find the gaps, and give you a plan to close them.","body":"A focused assessment or a CBP audit notice is not the time to find out your records are thin. We pull a cross-section of your entries, test your classifications and valuation, check your recordkeeping, and hand you a written report ranked by risk. Then we help you fix what matters first.","startingFee":"From $3,500","turnaround":"2 to 4 weeks","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"p6v9g96ZcinfllJMlN07f"}},"displayOrder":6}

{"title":"Tariff Classification Review","slug":"tariff-classification-review","summary":"We review your products and assign the correct Harmonized Tariff Schedule code so your entries clear without surprises.","body":"Getting the HTS code wrong costs you money and invites a CBP penalty. We look at how a product is made, what it does, and the notes that govern its heading, then we give you a defensible classification you can use on every entry. If the right code is unclear, we will tell you that too and walk you through your options.","startingFee":"$450 per product line","turnaround":"3 to 5 business days","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"g1FhQJJBhMmIdOrtoPZVR5V"}},"displayOrder":1}

{"title":"Customs Entry Filing","slug":"customs-entry-filing","summary":"As a licensed customs broker, we file your formal entries with CBP and get your goods released.","body":"We prepare and transmit your entry, calculate duties and fees, and coordinate with CBP and any partner government agency until your goods are released. License #28471 stands behind every filing. You get a single point of contact who actually answers the phone.","startingFee":"$125 per entry","turnaround":"Same day to next day","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"cTCkjuCs37SFAruHNMsRP"}},"displayOrder":5}

{"title":"Importer Security Filing","slug":"importer-security-filing","summary":"We file your ISF on time so your ocean freight is not held or fined at the port.","body":"The ISF, also called the 10 plus 2, has to be filed with CBP at least 24 hours before your cargo is loaded overseas. Miss it and you face a $5,000 penalty per filing and possible holds. We collect the data from your supplier and forwarder and file it correctly, every shipment.","startingFee":"$45 per filing","turnaround":"Same day","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"uY4Z4o48RUxr0UXPOiiZtq"}},"displayOrder":4}

{"title":"Duty Drawback Claim Filing","slug":"duty-drawback-claim-filing","summary":"If you import goods and later export them, you may be owed a refund of the duties you paid. We file the claim.","body":"Duty drawback lets you recover up to 99 percent of duties on goods that are exported or destroyed. Most importers never claim it because the paperwork is heavy. We match your import and export records, build the claim, and file it with CBP. You only move forward once we confirm there is money worth chasing.","startingFee":"Contingency or flat fee","turnaround":"Varies by claim volume","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"nBOhBloRqdl6F72vBZX33L"}},"displayOrder":3}

{"title":"Conference and Training Workshop","slug":"conference-and-training-workshop","summary":"On-site or virtual training so your team understands classification, valuation, and recordkeeping.","body":"We run half-day and full-day workshops for import and logistics teams. Topics include HTS classification, customs valuation, USMCA, ISF, and recordkeeping duties. Sessions use your own products and entries so the training sticks. We also speak at trade association events.","startingFee":"From $2,500 per session","turnaround":"Scheduled","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"gUT7v281goj4qmd4NEvbmM"}},"displayOrder":9}

{"title":"Monthly Compliance Retainer","slug":"monthly-compliance-retainer","summary":"Ongoing broker and compliance support for importers who want a trade desk without hiring one.","body":"For a flat monthly fee you get a named specialist, classification and ISF support, entry oversight, and a standing call when you need it. It works well for importers doing steady volume who do not have trade staff in house. No long contract. Cancel with 30 days notice.","startingFee":"From $1,500 per month","turnaround":"Ongoing","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"qwfulh06zPAHuBDsJvQL5q"}},"displayOrder":8}

{"title":"HTS Reclassification Opinion","slug":"hts-reclassification-opinion","summary":"Think you are paying duty under the wrong code? We give you a written opinion you can stand behind.","body":"Sometimes a product has been classified the same way for years and nobody checked whether it was right. We research the headings, the explanatory notes, and prior CBP rulings, then issue a written opinion on the correct classification. If it lowers your duty, we tell you how to fix past entries through a protest or prior disclosure.","startingFee":"From $1,200","turnaround":"1 to 2 weeks","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"zRz91uIz2J0b6U16nKW1ID"}},"displayOrder":7}

{"title":"USMCA Rule of Origin Analysis","slug":"usmca-rule-of-origin-analysis","summary":"We work through the USMCA rules of origin for your goods and tell you whether they qualify for duty-free treatment.","body":"Qualifying under USMCA is not a guess. We run the tariff shift and regional value content tests against your bill of materials, document the math, and prepare the certification of origin if your goods qualify. If they do not qualify, we show you what would have to change.","startingFee":"$900 per product","turnaround":"1 to 2 weeks","heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"ciBnpE2S3fK5LZXgMKHmLI"}},"displayOrder":2}
